name: Sourdough Buttermilk Biscuits and Scones
recipeIngredient: 1 teaspoon Soda (1 1/2 or 2 teaspoons if starter is real sour)
recipeInstructions:
1. The night before, or three to four hours before you intend to prepare these, combine the sourdough start, buttermilk and 1 cup of the flour in a large bowl.
2. Cover and let stand at room temperature.
3. When ready to use, add egg and oil; stir well.
4. Combine sugar, salt, baking powder and soda; sprinkle over top of batter.
5. Fold in as much of the remaining flour as needed to make a dough you can still stir (2 to 3 cups should do it).
6. To make Scones, dump the batter out on a heavily floured board. Sprinkle flour over the top and roll out. Cut out scones.
7. Allow scones to rise for a few minutes, then fry both sides in hot oil until golden brown.
8. For Biscuits, add enough flour to make moderately stiff so you can knead dough. Knead for a few minutes, then roll and cut out biscuits.
9. Drop the biscuits on both sides in oil or melted butter and place them on a cookie sheet.
10. Let rise for an hour, then bake at 375 degrees for 25 minutes.
